manifest.name: Task Accountability Tracker

name: Task Accountability Tracker

releases.author: SattaRIP
releases.description:
# v1.0.0 - Initial Release

First release of Task Accountability Tracker!

## Features

- 📊 **Track daily note engagement** - See Week/Month/Year views of your engagement
- 🔥 **Streak tracking** - Monitor your current streak and longest streak
- 🔄 **Auto-detect Daily Notes folder** - Automatically finds your folder from Daily Notes or Periodic Notes plugin settings
- ⚙️ **Customizable engagement threshold** - Set your own minimum (default: 3 checked tasks)
- 🔍 **Detailed breakdown** - See exactly which days you missed and why (missing files vs. low engagement)

## Requirements

- Works with **Daily Notes** (core plugin) or **Periodic Notes** (community plugin)
- Daily notes must have dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format in the filename

## Installation

Once approved, search for "Task Accountability Tracker" in Obsidian Settings → Community Plugins

## How to Use

1. Click the bar chart icon (📊) in the left ribbon
2. View your engagement statistics
3. Switch between Week, Month, and Year views
4. Check the breakdown to see which days you missed

## Settings

- Daily notes folder path (auto-detected)
- Engagement threshold (default: 3)
- Default period (Week/Month/Year)
- Period type (Rolling vs Calendar)

## Philosophy

This plugin helps you build the habit of daily engagement with your notes by showing you:
- Which days you didn't engage
- Your current streak (motivation!)
- Patterns in your productivity

It's not about perfection—it's about consistency.
releases.name: v1.0.0 - Initial Release
